human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for automotive-parts specificity, authority-source quality, lifecycle-state clarity, traceability and release boundary handling, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to automotive parts execution, sequence and kit readiness, part-revision integrity, container and label evidence, blocked-work escalation, and completion evidence.

Authority sources cover IATF customer-specific requirements and MAQMSR, AIAG core tools and MMOG/LE, GS1 traceability and EPCIS or CBV standards, Odette labeling guidance, and NHTSA recall-boundary guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope execution, missing prerequisites, engineering and supplier boundary rejection, traceability conflicts, quality holds, unsupported completion, conflicting-policy escalation, execution exceptions, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/automotive-and-mobility/parts-operations-specialist/ so automotive parts operations remain distinct from adjacent quality, production planning, inventory, procurement, supplier-quality, and logistics lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around final quality release, engineering change approval, supplier approval, sourcing decisions, recall handling, and safety authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated automotive parts operations specialist for sequence readiness, part execution, blocked-work handling, traceability reconciliation, quality-boundary escalation, and completion evidence capture.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
